response.justification: The candidate is a web and full-stack developer whose core expertise is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — not C++ or Rust. Their C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 writing components for an IDE and a bachelor's thesis project; neither demonstrates the modern, production-grade C++ proficiency the role demands. While the GISCollective and Green Map work shows genuine geospatial interest and the Dunnhumby role involved high-throughput, low-latency systems, these positives are not sufficient to offset the absence of the primary technical requirements for a routing data pipeline position.
strengths: Real geospatial domain experience via GISCollective (event-driven geo-data platform) and Green Map GIS application, including a FOSS4G conference talk
Demonstrated low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby using asynchronous I/O, caching, and fibers in DLang handling millions of requests per second
Broad Linux and distributed/micro-services exposure across multiple roles, including Kubernetes deployment and CI/CD infrastructure
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over a decade old (NI LabWindows, 2014–2015); no evidence of modern C++ usage in production routing or geospatial pipelines
No Rust experience listed anywhere in the resume or skills section
Career trajectory has moved consistently away from systems programming toward TypeScript/Node.js web development and AI tooling, making a pivot to C++ routing pipelines a significant stretch
